human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for food-service supplier-coordination specificity, receiving and traceability dependencies, pricing and refund boundaries, fraud and safety escalation, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to supplier coordination, receiving and traceability handoffs, blocker tracking, customer-impact routing, and closure confirmation rather than generic procurement or guest service.
Authority sources cover FTC order and fraud guidance, DOJ hospitality reservation rules, NIST price-verification standards, FDA Food Code and traceability guidance, and GS1 US foodservice standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing prerequisites, duplicate ownership, booking or payment conflicts, refund pressure, safety stops, high-volume intake, execution-boundary rejection, terminology normalization, low-confidence handling, fraud escalation, conflicting-policy escalation, and closure confirmation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-service-and-restaurants/supplier-coordination-food-service-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent guest-services, front-office, pricing, fraud, culinary, and operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
Research-summary records no blocking unresolved ambiguity; remaining ambiguity is explicitly tenant or jurisdiction retrieval work.
No retained-implementation runtime is claimed in this specification-based package.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated supplier coordination specialist for dependency intake, handoff packaging, blocker escalation, customer-exception routing, and closure confirmation in food service and restaurants.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
